馬

The year turns

Year of the Fire Horse

2026. Once in sixty years the Horse catches fire, a year that rewards the bold and scorches the hesitant. If the Horse is yours, this is your decade's hinge.

Read the Fire Horse

0 of 15 chapters

♈ Aries and 牛 Year of the Ox

Sun Bear

You trust your ability to climb more than you trust safe ground.

Zodi Animal · No. 002 / 144

In brief

Your Primal Zodiac Animal is the Sun Bear, born where Aries crosses the Year of the Ox.

  • Aries

    gives you the nerve to begin before you feel ready.

  • The Ox

    gives you the strength to remain long after the excitement fades.

  • The Sun Bear

    turns both into one instinct: climb past the point everyone else calls the limit.

One strength, one cost: the same nature that carries you up alone can forget how to let anyone climb with you. This reading traces where that instinct comes from, and what it becomes when it grows up.

Meet the Sun Bear

The smallest bear on Earth, and undaunted by it

A compact climber of Southeast Asian forests, marked by a gold crescent like a rising sun.

Everything this reading says about you is grounded in what the Sun Bear actually is: a small body that climbs where heavier animals can't, a bright crescent worn openly on its chest, a life mostly handled alone. This chapter is that foundation. The rest of the page draws meaning from these real traits.

You do not have to be bigger or louder. Your edge begins the moment you trust your own nature.

6 traits below

01of six

Sun crescent

Animal fact

The sun bear is named for the pale gold crescent across its chest, shaped like a rising sun. No two bears wear the same one, and it is the single visible thing that tells one bear from the next.

Our reading

You carry one true mark of who you are, and when you let it show, people know they are seeing the real you.

02of six

Curved claws

Animal fact

Long, sickle-shaped claws and a bite strong for its size let the sun bear tear open a bee nest or a rotten log and grip bark on a straight vertical climb.

Our reading

You come equipped. When something is worth reaching for, you already have what it takes to get a grip and hold on.

03of six

Long tongue

Animal fact

An unusually long tongue, commonly cited at twenty to twenty-five centimetres, lets the sun bear reach honey and insects from a crack no other bear could work.

Our reading

You get at what others give up on. Where a problem looks sealed shut, you keep working it until it opens.

04of six

Smallest bear

Animal fact

The sun bear is the smallest of the world's bear species, yet it takes on climbs and nests that would daunt animals far larger than itself.

Our reading

Your size has never set your ceiling. You read a big risk as information, and you would rather be the smallest thing at the top than the safest one on the ground.

05of six

Tree climber

Animal fact

A strong climber, the sun bear sleeps and forages high in the forest, going straight up a trunk to a bee nest forty feet off the ground and staying on the wall past the first sting.

Our reading

Facing something hard, your instinct is to go straight at it. You see the height as an invitation, and that is a rare kind of nerve.

06of six

Solitary

Animal fact

The sun bear lives largely alone in the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, foraging and denning by itself rather than in a group.

Our reading

You are steady on your own, and that steadiness is a gift to everyone who gets to lean on it.

Two zodiacs, one animal

Where Aries and the Ox meet

Aries brings

  • The first move, taken before permission arrives
  • Appetite for the tallest target in view
  • Speed of decision once convinced

The Ox brings

  • Tolerance for long, unglamorous effort
  • Loyalty to the chosen path
  • Resistance to hurry and to outside pressure

What the crossing makes

Cross them and the two instincts close into one. You get an animal that picks the hardest target in the room, commits fast, and climbs past the point everyone else calls the limit. It looks compact and quiet from the outside. Inside, it is all commitment.

Where they pull against each other

Aries wants movement now; the Ox wants certainty first. One ignites before the route is proven; the other refuses to abandon a route once taken. Fused well, that is commitment. Fused badly, it is starting too fast and staying too long, over-investment wearing the costume of loyalty. The chapters ahead are largely about telling those two apart.

Drag the dial to weigh your two halves and watch the blend change

Aries
Ox
50 Aries In balance Ox 50

In balance, you are quick enough to begin and grounded enough to finish. You do not ignore risk; you study it, measure it, and keep moving while the path is still worth taking.

Your element

土

Your element is Earth

Earth's archetype: The Nurturer · in the Sun Bear: The Steady Builder

Earth's archetype in this system is the Nurturer. In the Sun Bear it hardens into something more specific: the Steady Builder: ground that holds while something tall gets made on it. Earth is the still point other people orbit. It stabilizes: reliability, practical judgment, patience, protection that does not announce itself, foundations that outlast moods. In excess it packs down hard: rigidity, over-responsibility, staying too long, carrying emotional weight silently. The Shadow chapter ahead wears this same signature.

How this was calculated

Every Zodi Animal takes its element from the fixed element of its Chinese year-animal: for the Ox, that is Earth. Your Western sign adds a second note: Aries carries Fire, and in the five-phase cycle Fire feeds Earth. That is why Earth is not just present in the Sun Bear but settled: the spark burns down into ground that holds. The pairing of animal and element archetype is Zodi Animal's own reading of these traditional systems, offered for reflection.

DirectionCenter
SeasonLate summer
VirtueFidelity
ColorYellow

The elements around you

Wu Xing 五行, the Five Phases, is a system of relationships, not isolated traits. Here is how the other four move around yours.

Generates you

火Fire nourishes you

Fire feeds Earth the way flame feeds ash back into soil. Warmth and encouragement from Fire-natured people keep your steadiness from turning into stagnation.

Balances you

木Wood keeps you from hardening

Wood is the root that pushes through packed ground. It gives your steadiness somewhere to grow instead of just somewhere to sit.

You generate

金You generate Metal

Earth is where Metal forms, compressed slowly out of stability and time. What you build patiently becomes the structure others draw on.

You steady

水You steady Water

Earth is the bank that holds a river to its course, giving Water's motion shape and direction instead of flood.

Go deeper: Bazi 八字

This is Wu Xing 五行, and it runs deeper

Your Earth comes from one place: the Ox, whose fixed element anchors the whole Sun Bear. But Fire, Wood, Metal, Water, and Earth are not fixed labels. They are a grammar for how energy feeds and wears on itself, and the Ox's Earth is only your starting note.

Bazi 八字, the Four Pillars, reads that grammar across your whole chart: year, month, day, and the exact hour you were born. The Sun Bear takes the element of just one pillar, your Ox year. Your full chart also carries the Fire that Aries lends, in proportions only your birth time can show.

BaziRead your Four Pillars ElementsSee all five phases

Go deeper: Chakras

Earth in the body

Held beside the yogic map, Earth finds its closest match in the Root chakra, Muladhara: grounding and belonging at the base of the spine. Its bija sound is LAM; its guardian, the elephant: steady, patient, unhurried in exactly Earth's way.

Treat the pairing as an interpretive bridge between two traditions, not an equivalence.

Root chakraMuladharaBija: LAMGuardian: elephant
ChakraVisit your Root chakra PracticeYoga for your element

Take it further

土ElementThe Earth element in full OverviewAll five elements Bazi · 八字Your Four Pillars Feng shuiSet up your workspace

The Sun Bear dossier

The Dossier

Zodi Animal
No. 002 / 144

☼ Sun Bear · Aries × Ox

The Solo Climber

First up the tree, too stubborn to come back down empty.

Profile

Archetype
The Solo Climber
Central gift
Going first at the tallest target
Central shadow
Climbing everything alone
Protective instinct
Go up and get it yourself
Growth lesson
Let someone hold the base
Power phase
Full Moon
Element
Earth
Ideal habitat
A quiet base with a clear path upward

These five traits are yours too. Take the test to see whether you climb the way the Sun Bear climbs, or whether you are a rare one who carries them differently.

Take the personality test

Tap a trait to read it

    Sun BearPick a trait above to see how the Sun Bear wears it, and which side it leans toward.

    The Primal Mirror

    How this lives inside you

    One mechanism, five states. Reflex, capacity, defense, distortion, integration.

    01 · Reflex

    Instinct: your first move

    You go up first, and you finish what you start.

    Before thought gets a vote, your attention has already picked the hardest thing in the room and started measuring it. Difficulty registers as invitation. Waiting registers as cost.

    Once you start, you do not come back down halfway. You would rather be underestimated than watched, and you trust your own read over anyone else's caution.

    02 · Capacity

    Gift: what you do better than most

    Your nerve gets the hard thing started, and your endurance keeps it moving.

    That same reflex, given air, becomes a rare combination: the nerve to begin what others postpone and the endurance to finish what others abandon. The slow, difficult work that needs one committed person is yours to carry.

    Because you make it look manageable, people lean on you without noticing the weight. That is the capacity. Hold that thought; it returns in the next two states.

    03 · Defense

    Guard: what you protect, and how

    You take the hit so the people behind you do not have to.

    What the gift is guarding is autonomy: momentum, competence, authorship of your own climb. You protect what matters by going first and absorbing the hard part yourself, rather than asking anyone else to carry the risk.

    You would rather deliver the result than narrate the struggle. It is real devotion. It is also a great deal to hold alone, and defenses this good do not know when to stand down.

    04 · Distortion

    Shadow: the guard, overgrown

    The solitude you built to prove your nerve can quietly turn into loneliness.

    What trips it. Being told the target is too high, being asked to wait, or someone stepping into the work uninvited.

    What your mind says. If I accept help, the achievement is no longer fully mine.

    What you do. You speed up, close the door, and finish before anyone can take part.

    What it costs. People learn there is no room for them until the work is done, so they stop offering. The independence you were proud of becomes a loneliness you never chose. A climb continued past its worth starts feeling like meaning when it is only momentum.

    In love

    You show devotion by handling it, then wonder why a partner only meets you at the end.

    At work

    You close ranks the moment someone joins uninvited, before deciding whether you wanted the company.

    With friends

    You arrive with it already solved, so no friend learns there was a place for them in it.

    05 · Integration

    Awakened: the same strength, integrated

    The nerve that carried you alone becomes the nerve to let someone in.

    The awakened Sun Bear still goes first, still sets the standard by doing the hard part, still finishes. What changes is the accounting: support stops reading as a debit against authorship. You choose worthy climbs instead of all climbs, and you can tell endurance-as-courage from endurance-as-fear of changing direction.

    You can let someone hold the base, and the climb is still yours.

    Name the form you are becoming

    Complete the sentence: I remain strong even when I

    Career and working life

    Your working nature

    An application of the archetype, not a verdict: you are likely to work best when no one stands over your shoulder and the timeline is measured in years, not weeks.

    01 / 05

    You lead from the front

    You lead by doing the hard part first, in front of everyone, before asking anyone else to do it. The standard is set with your hands, not a memo.

    People follow because they watched you go first into the part of the project nobody wanted, and come out with something to show. Results are your argument; you rarely need another.

    02 / 05

    A mandate, a runway, and room

    A clear outcome you own, a long runway, and nobody hovering at the finish line. That is the setup for your best, most patient work.

    Micromanagement flattens you fast. So does a crowded structure where three people watch one task. Environments that drain this animal: open-plan constant interruption, short sprint horizons, consensus required for every move.

    • High autonomy
    • Clear ownership
    • Long timelines
    • Minimal oversight

    03 / 05

    Fast to commit, slow to hand off

    Your decision pattern is Aries-shaped: quick once convinced, and convinced by your own read more than by argument. That serves you when the data is thin and punishes you when the sunk cost is thick. Schedule one deliberate second look partway up any long climb.

    Your delegation pattern is the Ox's: hand off the easy edges, keep the core. Practice the reverse once a quarter: give someone a real piece of the middle, with authorship, and watch what it buys you.

    04 / 05

    Two ways the momentum breaks

    The first: someone steps into your lane uninvited. It reads as intrusion, not help, and you pull back rather than share.

    The second is quieter and more dangerous: refusing help so long that the weight isolates you. Endurance becomes a slow burnout nobody around you can see coming. The burnout pattern of this profile is not collapse but withdrawal that looks like diligence.

    05 / 05

    Built to last decades, if you pace it

    Your edge is endurance, not speed; the long game is protecting it. Choose one or two people you trust and let them in fully: real parts of the load, not the easy tasks.

    One growth practice: delegate on purpose, not as a last resort. And build actual recovery into the schedule: the boring maintenance of a body and mind expected to keep working for thirty more years. Treat it like the distance event it is.

    Your Ideal Habitat

    Living with your Zodi Animal

    A quiet base with a clear path upward.

    The sun bear rests high, forages alone, and keeps one route in mind. Its human translation: privacy without isolation, stability without stagnation, and always one visible next climb. Every recommendation below follows from that: warm earth materials because Earth is your element, vertical lines because height is your direction, low clutter because a climber travels light.

    Your feng shui palette

    Feng shui builds a room from your element and the one that feeds it. Earth is your ground; Fire warms it to life. The bar shows roughly how much of each to use, and every color carries a light and a dark tint, so you can build an airy room or a den that holds you.

    Most of the roomA small accent

    GroundEarthAbout 46% of the room

    Forest floor umber

    Your own Earth tone, deep and stable. The ground the room rests on: floors, large furniture, walls.

    SupportEarthAbout 28% of the room

    Sun crescent gold

    Earth's warm yellow, the everyday supporting tone. Textiles, rugs, mid-size pieces, and the light in the room.

    AccentFireAbout 17% of the room

    Honeycomb amber

    Fire feeds Earth, so this amber is the spark that keeps steadiness from going stale. Cushions, art, smaller pieces.

    SparkFireAbout 9% of the room

    Ember red

    Concentrated Fire, the strongest note. One or two small touches, so it warms the room without scorching your calm.

    Arrange your whole space with feng shui

    Workspace

    A solid desk with a wall at your back and one tall focal point above eye level, the climb you set yourself daily. This is the focused, solitary-work post the animal needs.

    Bedroom

    Low and den-like in warm earth tones, one textured surface underhand. The protected resting area: the bear sleeps above ground, you sleep behind quiet.

    Entry

    A low threshold with real texture underfoot and one clear line of movement through it. Grounding on the way in, a clear route on the way out.

    Living area

    A tucked, warm corner near the group but shelled enough to be your own, where trusted people can enter without taking over.

    Planning nook

    One small surface holding a single visible symbol of the next climb: the sketch, the application, the plan. One, not five. The Sun Bear keeps one route in mind.

    Reflection

    A quiet spot with one still bowl of water for the new-moon practice of naming the next climb before you start it.

    Elements to avoid

    • Visual clutter along your main path; a climber travels light
    • Competing tall features in one room; one summit per space
    • Cold, echoing surfaces everywhere; Earth needs warmth and texture
    • Workstations facing away from the door; the guard never rests there
    The five-minute reset

    Five-minute habitat reset: clear the surface you see first when you enter, set one object that stands for the current climb where daylight hits it, and put something warm within reach of where you sit: wood, wool, clay.

    Free with a Zodi Animal account

    Redesign your space with feng shui

    A room-by-room guide tuned to your Earth element: arrange your home so it works with your nature instead of against it. Yours free when you make a Zodi Animal account.

    Get the free eBook

    Feng shui elements here are symbolic. They support intention and act as visual reminders, not claims that any object, color, or direction produces wealth, health, romance, or success.

    Integration

    Practices for the climb

    Small, physical, repeatable. One from each room of your life.

    • The lesson

      Let someone hold the base. Authorship survives assistance; the reading has been building to this one sentence.

    • A ritual

      At each new moon, name the next climb out loud, one sentence, one target, before you start it. Choosing worthy climbs is the awakened form of taking all of them.

    • In your space

      Keep exactly one symbol of the current climb visible, and retire it ceremonially when the climb is done. The retirement matters as much as the display.

    • In one bond

      This week, tell one person what you are carrying while you are still carrying it. Not for rescue. For witness.

    • At work

      Hand one real piece of the middle, not the edges, to someone you trust, with full authorship. Watch whether the result is worse, or just differently yours.

    Keeper Stones

    Four stones keep this animal

    Each one answers a different Sun Bear tension. Pick one up; the one you choose stays on your card.

    Anchor stone

    Tiger's eye

    Why it belongs here

    Banded gold and brown: bark and crescent in one stone. It belongs to the Sun Bear because it holds the animal's two colors and its two speeds.

    What it supports

    Discernment between a meaningful climb and an unnecessary struggle, the exact judgment the signature Rootedness axis makes hard.

    A simple use

    Hold it while naming the next climb at the new moon; if the reason sounds like pride rather than purpose, choose again.

    Care

    Durable; keep from harsh chemicals.

    Clarity stone

    Smoky quartz

    Why it belongs here

    Grounding, the color of forest shade: the pause partway up.

    What it supports

    Staying clear-headed mid-climb instead of pushing on blind; the deliberate second look the work chapter prescribes.

    A simple use

    Keep it on the desk as the physical reminder of the halfway check.

    Care

    Stable; avoid prolonged sun.

    Courage stone

    Carnelian

    Why it belongs here

    Warm and quick: the Aries half of the crossing in mineral form.

    What it supports

    The first move at a target that looks too high, for the days the Ox's caution wins arguments it should lose.

    A simple use

    Carry it on days that require beginning, not finishing.

    Care

    Color can fade in strong sun.

    Boundary stone

    Black tourmaline

    Why it belongs here

    A protective stone for a climb taken alone.

    What it supports

    Keeping solitude chosen rather than defaulted, the shadow's specific imbalance.

    A simple use

    Place it at the entry: the threshold where you decide who climbs with you.

    Care

    Safe to handle; rinse and dry.

    Stones are cultural and symbolic tools, not medical treatment. Some fade in sunlight or dislike water; follow each care note.

    Moon rhythm

    Your power moon

    The full moon is the month's summit: the climb of light completed, everything visible at once. It fits the Sun Bear because this is the one animal whose work is usually finished in the dark, alone; the full moon is the standing invitation to finish something in the open instead. What to do: the hard thing, in daylight, with one person watching. What to avoid: starting three new climbs because the light makes everything look reachable.

    Your next full moon · Power Moon

    Wednesday, July 29, 2026

    21 days from today.

    When it rises, do the hard thing in daylight. Then let one person watch you do it. The climb was never the problem for you. Being seen at the base is.

    1. Tue Jul 28The rise. Choose the climb no one else will touch, and start it before you feel ready.
    2. Wed Jul 29 · Full MoonThe peak. Do the hard thing in daylight, and let one person watch you do it.
    3. Thu Jul 30The hold. Finish what you started on the rise instead of reaching for a new trunk.
    Explore the Moon hub

    The proverb of your year

    積jī少shǎo成chéng多duō

    Tap a character to hear it

    Literally accumulate few, become many

    Meaning Many small efforts gathered steadily become much; what looks too big is built grain by grain.

    The nest forty feet up is not reached in one leap. It is reached one claw-hold at a time, by a bear too small to have any business up there and too stubborn to come back down. What you are building is bigger than your size. Build it grain by grain.

    A proverb we tie to the Year of the Ox. The pairing is our own reading, not tradition.

    In relationship

    How the Sun Bear loves

    You show love by carrying weight, but you may forget to let others carry you.

    Care, for you, is action: quiet protection, reliability, arriving with the problem already handled. The pattern underneath shapes every bond differently: asking is hard, vulnerability waits for proof, and the capable one is rarely offered help.

    How you show love

    By doing, not declaring. A partner feels your devotion in effort: the thing fixed before they mentioned it twice, the weight lifted before they asked.

    What makes you feel safe

    Being trusted with the hard thing, and not being managed while you do it. Softness offered without being framed as rescue.

    What they may misunderstand

    Your self-sufficiency can read as not needing them. You do. You need to be reached mid-climb, not only met at the top.

    Your conflict pattern

    You go quiet and handle it, then resent handling it alone, a resentment the other person never got the chance to prevent.

    What to practice

    Say the sentence before the summit: name what you are carrying while you are still carrying it.

    How you show up

    First into the intimidating thing without being asked, and you would rather arrive with the honey in hand than announce the plan.

    What they may misunderstand

    Because nothing ever looks heavy on you, friends assume no help is wanted. Then you quietly count who never offered.

    What to practice

    Let a friend stand at the base of one climb instead of finding it finished. They learn there was a place for them; you learn company is not interference.

    How you show up

    You take the family's tallest problem without being asked twice, and carry it so smoothly the family never sees the weight leave the ground.

    What they may misunderstand

    Ease. The cost is invisible by design, and the design is yours.

    What to practice

    Let them see the cost once. People can only love the effort they are allowed to witness.

    How you show up

    The first mover on the guarded project nobody wants, best with a long runway and no one rushing the finish.

    Your conflict pattern

    Someone climbs the same tree beside you uninvited and you close the gate on instinct.

    What to practice

    Say when you do want company, before the instinct answers for you.

    Healthy support, for this animal, is specific: people who admire your strength and still ask about the sting. Choose them over the ones who only applaud from the ground. Receiving care does not reduce independence; it is what makes thirty more years of it possible.

    Test this bond with a second birth date

    Computed across all 143 other crossings

    Matches the wheel favors

    Strongest pull

    Match I · of three

    火

    Loggerhead Shrike

    ♈ Aries × 蛇 Snake

    1953, 1965, 1977, 1989, 2001, 2013, 2025

    Same trineTheir Fire feeds your Earth

    The Snake shares the Ox's trine, so effort between you compounds. Their Fire feeds your Earth: this one is fuel.

    Read the Loggerhead Shrike

    Match II · of three

    金

    Fork-tailed Drongo

    ♊ Gemini × 鸡 Rooster

    1945, 1957, 1969, 1981, 1993, 2005, 2017

    Same trineYour Earth feeds their Metal

    The Rooster shares the Ox's trine, so effort between you compounds. Your Earth feeds their Metal: you are the warmth in it.

    Read the Fork-tailed Drongo

    Match III · of three

    水

    Golden Lion Tamarin

    ♌ Leo × 鼠 Rat

    1948, 1960, 1972, 1984, 1996, 2008, 2020

    Secret friendsWater nature

    The Rat is the Ox's secret friend, the wheel's quiet allied pair.

    Read the Golden Lion Tamarin

    Aries Ox horoscope

    Your Sun Bear horoscope

    Today, in one line: a door you have kept shut is worth opening an inch.

    Read today's and this week's reading, see how the Aries Ox matches with all twelve signs, and get answers to the questions people ask most about this crossing.

    Open the Sun Bear horoscope
    Today and this weekCompatibilityCommon questions

    Continue your climb

    Where to go from the Sun Bear

    Three matches surfaced above; the wheel holds 140 more. Each crossing opens onto others, and these are meanings to explore, not verdicts. Contrast is a mirror for self-knowledge, never a warning.

    Best next reading

    See how Aries changes across the other eleven years

    You have watched Aries fuse with the Ox. The fastest way to feel the system's logic is to watch the same spark land in eleven different bodies.

    All Aries animals

    Animals your instincts trust on sight

    Independent starters with long grips: kinship, not similarity.

    FossaAries · Year of the Rat Loggerhead ShrikeAries · Year of the Snake CassowaryAries · Year of the Rooster

    The temperaments that stretch yours

    Speed without grip, and grip without ground: your pattern, inverted.

    PronghornAries · Year of the Horse WallcreeperAries · Year of the Goat

    Same year, other skies

    The Ox's endurance under eleven other suns.

    Giant ClamTaurus · Year of the Ox Three-wattled BellbirdGemini · Year of the Ox Asian ElephantCancer · Year of the Ox Spotted HyenaLeo · Year of the Ox

    Same sky, other years

    Aries nerve wearing eleven other bodies.

    The last chapter

    The Sun Bear is yours.
    Now find out who climbs beside you.

    The Bond Test

    Bring a friend into the reading

    One birthdate is all it takes. Turn the wheel to their year, and their animal steps out to meet the Sun Bear.

    Your first friend

    01 The year they were born
    牛 1995 · Year of the Pig
    02 The month
    03 The day

    The Moon tonightCome back at the full moon, your power phase, and spend it in the open. Wander the MenagerieAll 144 crossings. Each one opens onto others.

    The Solo Climber · Aries × Ox · Earth 土

    Your line to keep

    I am the Sun Bear: first up the tree, built to finish, learning to let someone hold the base.

    熊

    Where you go next

    Three doors are open. Pick one.

    You know the shape of the Sun Bear now. Here is what the reading did not tell you yet, and where each thread runs.

    Love

    Who the Sun Bear is built to love

    You climb alone by instinct. One animal steadies the base without asking you to come down.

    It is not the one that climbs beside you. It is the one that stays at the bottom and does not flinch.

    See who matches     Bonds

    Test a friend against your wheel

    Drop any name onto the Sun Bear wheel and see where they sit. Ally, mirror, or friction.

    Some people feed the climb. Some quietly hold you at the base. The wheel tells you which.

    Run the Bond Test     Element 土

    Your Earth runs through eleven other animals

    The Sun Bear is one of twelve carrying Earth. The other eleven share your ground and bend it differently.

    Same element, twelve temperaments. A few will feel like family the moment you read them.

    See the Earth twelve

    Keep the thread before it closes.

    A free account saves this reading, your line, and the animals you test. Come back to the exact page you left, not a blank start.

    Takes about a minute. No card. You can delete it anytime.

    Opens the share sheet.

    Sun Bear · 002 / 144 · Earth 土

    From your Zodi Animal reading

    Send it out

    Keep every reading you are given.

    Make it permanent

    This device will remember the Sun Bear until it doesn't. A free account keeps your animal, your reflections, and every reading you are given, renewed with each new moon, on any device.

    • Your Sun Bear, saved for good
    • Your identity line and keeper stone, kept
    • The feng shui eBook for your element, free
    Create your free account

    The Sun Bear Test

    Do you climb the way the Sun Bear climbs?

    The Sun Bear is built from five traits. Ten quick questions measure yours against its shape and tell you whether you are a true Sun Bear, or a rare one who carries them differently.

    5 traits 10 questions ≈ 2 minutes No account needed

      Sun Bear You

      How this reading is built

      Each Zodi Animal reading combines four threads: Western zodiac symbolism (your Sun sign), Chinese zodiac symbolism (your year animal), five-element interpretation (Wu Xing, via the year-animal's fixed element), and the real biology and behavior of the animal itself. The archetype that binds them, here the Solo Climber, is Zodi Animal's own framework. Readings are for reflection and self-exploration; they are not clinical assessment, scientific personality testing, or a claim about fate. Animal facts are drawn from zoological references and flagged in the text as Animal fact; interpretive material is flagged as Our reading.

      • IUCN Red List · Helarctos malayanus
      • Smithsonian's National Zoo · Sun bear

      Last reviewed 2026-07-06

      The Primal Zodiac, 144 animals

      Zodi Animal

      One hundred and forty-four animals, born where the Western Sun signs cross the Chinese zodiac years. A slow tradition of meaning, kept under the moon.

      Find your Zodi Animal ✦

      The Daily Reading

      Your reading, at dawn.

      One short passage for your Zodi Animal each morning. The moon's phase, what it favors, and a line meant for you. Free, and it takes ten seconds to join.

      No noise. Unsubscribe anytime.

      守shǒu得dé雲yún開kāi見jiàn月yuè明míng

      Hold on until the clouds part, and the bright moon appears.

      From the Proverb Pond
